tasks.name: Request the earliest weekday speech evaluation after 3 PM at the Main Clinic for a 5-year-old
tasks.description: From the speech therapy section, submit a booking request for an initial evaluation for a 5-year-old at the Main Clinic, choosing the first available weekday appointment between May 10 and May 20 that starts at or after 3:00 PM.
steps: Navigate to the website homepage.
Click on the main navigation menu item labeled "Services."
From the services list, click on "Speech Therapy" to open the speech therapy service page.
On the speech therapy page, click the button labeled "Schedule an Evaluation."
In the booking form, open the "Service Type" dropdown and select "Initial Speech Evaluation."
In the child age field, type "5".
Open the date picker and select the first weekday appointment date that appears between May 10 and May 20 on the calendar.
In the time selection area, choose the earliest available time slot that starts at or after "3:00 PM."

In the location dropdown, select "Main Clinic - Downtown," then fill in the parent name, phone number, and email fields with sample information (e.g., name "Jamie Rivera," phone "555-123-4567," email "jamie@example.com") and click the "Submit Request" button.

tasks.name: Choose the autism therapist for teens with more experience and request an appointment
tasks.description: Use the therapist directory to find therapists specializing in autism for teens, compare the first two in the list by years of experience, add the more experienced therapist to favorites, and submit an appointment request for that therapist.
steps: Navigate to the website homepage.
Click on the main navigation item labeled "Our Team" or "Therapists" to open the therapist directory.
In the filters panel, select the specialty filter option "Autism" and the age group filter option "Teens (13–17)."
Open the sort dropdown and select "Years of Experience (Most to Least)" to reorder the results.
Click on the first therapist card in the sorted list to open their profile page and note the number of years of experience displayed in the overview section.
Use the browser back button or directory link to return to the therapist list and click on the second therapist card to open their profile page and note the number of years of experience shown there.

Decide which therapist has more years of experience, then navigate to that therapist’s profile (using back navigation or the directory if needed) and click the "Add to Favorites" or heart icon on their profile.
In the site header or account tools area, click on "Favorites" or "Saved Therapists" to open the favorites list.
In the favorites list, locate the chosen therapist and click the button labeled "Request Appointment" or "Book with This Therapist."

In the appointment request form that appears, type a child name such as "Taylor Chen" in the child name field, select a preferred day (e.g., "Wednesday") and time range (e.g., "After 4 PM") from the dropdowns, and click the "Send Request" button.

tasks.name: Save three sensory processing articles for ages 3–5 that take at least 5 minutes to read
tasks.description: From the parent resources library, find and save three articles about sensory processing aimed at ages 3–5 where each article has a listed reading time of 5 minutes or longer, then open the saved reading list page.
steps: Navigate to the website homepage.
Click on the main navigation item labeled "Resources" or "Parent Library" to open the resources section.
In the resources search bar, type "sensory processing" and click the search icon or press Enter.
In the filters sidebar, select the age group filter option "3–5 years" and in the content type filter, select "Articles" while deselecting other content types such as videos or podcasts.
In the reading time or length filter, choose the option indicating a minimum of "5 minutes" (for example, select "5–10 minutes" or enter "5" in a minimum minutes field).

Scroll through the results and locate the first article card that shows a reading time label of "5 min read" or longer, then click the "Save," bookmark, or "Add to Reading List" icon on that article card.
Continue scrolling down the same results page to find a second article about sensory processing with a reading time of at least 5 minutes and click its "Save" or bookmark icon.

If necessary, click the "Next" or page 2 button at the bottom of the results list and save a third article with the same topic and minimum 5-minute reading time by clicking its "Save" or bookmark icon.
In the site header or account tools area, click on "My Reading List" or "Saved Resources" to open the page showing the articles you saved.

tasks.name: Register for a social skills group next month for ages 8–10 costing under $50
tasks.description: Find a social skills group for children aged 8–10 scheduled for next calendar month with a listed price under $50, then complete and submit the registration form for the earliest starting qualifying group.
steps: Navigate to the website homepage.
Click on the navigation item labeled "Groups & Classes" or "Group Programs" to open the groups listing page.
In the groups search bar, type "social skills" and press Enter.
In the age group filter, select the option for "8–10 years" and in the date range filter choose the next full calendar month (for example, if it is March now, select "April 1" to "April 30").
In the price filter or maximum price field, enter "50" as the maximum and apply the filter to show only groups with a price of $50 or less.
Open the sort dropdown and select "Start Date (Soonest First)" to order the social skills groups.
Click on the first group card in the filtered and sorted list to open the group details page.
On the group details page, click the button labeled "Register" or "Sign Up."

In the registration form, type a child name such as "Lena Ortiz" and age "9," select a listed session time (for example, "Tuesdays 4:00 PM") from the time slot dropdown, fill in the parent contact fields (name, phone, email) with sample values, and click the "Submit Registration" button.

tasks.name: Estimate monthly cost for 4 speech and 2 OT sessions with an in-network plan and request a detailed quote
tasks.description: Use the cost estimator to calculate the estimated monthly fee for 4 speech therapy sessions and 2 occupational therapy sessions under a selected in-network insurance plan, then send a contact form asking for a detailed monthly quote using those exact quantities.
steps: Navigate to the website homepage.
Click on the navigation item labeled "Pricing & Insurance" to open the pricing information page.
Scroll to the section titled "Cost Estimator" and click the button labeled "Open Calculator" or similar.
In the calculator, open the service dropdown, select "Speech Therapy," and enter "4" in the field for sessions per month.
Click the button or link labeled "Add Another Service," choose "Occupational Therapy" from the dropdown, and enter "2" in the sessions-per-month field for this second service.
In the insurance dropdown, select an option labeled as in-network, such as "BrightCare Plus (In-Network)."
Click the "Calculate Estimate" button to generate the estimated monthly total.
Below the estimate, click the link or button labeled "Request Detailed Quote" or "Talk to Us About Costs."

In the contact form that opens, enter a name such as "Jordan Patel" and an email address like "jordan@example.com," and in the message textbox type that you want a detailed monthly quote for "4 speech therapy sessions and 2 occupational therapy sessions per month with the BrightCare Plus in-network plan," then click the "Send Request" button.

tasks.name: Find the nearest clinic within 15 miles of ZIP 94110 with free parking and Saturday hours and request a call
tasks.description: Use the locations finder to search near ZIP code 94110, filter for clinics within 15 miles that have free parking and are open on Saturdays, select the nearest result, and submit a callback request form for that location.
steps: Navigate to the website homepage.
Click on the navigation item labeled "Locations" or "Clinics" to open the locations finder page.
In the ZIP code search field, type "94110" and click the "Search" button.
In the distance filter dropdown, select "Within 15 miles."
In the amenities or features filter area, check the option labeled "Free Parking."
In the hours filter, check the option labeled "Open Saturdays" or select "Saturday" under available days.
Open the sort dropdown for search results and select "Distance (Nearest First)."
Click on the first clinic card in the filtered and sorted results list to open that location’s detail page.
On the location detail page, click the button labeled "Request a Call" or "Have Us Call You."

In the callback request form, enter a phone number such as "415-555-0199," choose a preferred call time from the dropdown (for example, "Morning 9 AM–12 PM"), optionally type a short note like "Question about Saturday appointments" in the message field, and click the "Submit" button.

tasks.name: Create and save a weekly therapy plan with 3 specific sessions for one child
tasks.description: Use the scheduling tool to build a weekly therapy plan for one child by adding three specific sessions (speech on Tuesday at 4:00 PM for 60 minutes, occupational therapy on Thursday at 5:30 PM for 60 minutes, and counseling on Saturday at 11:00 AM for 45 minutes) and save the plan with a custom name.
steps: Navigate to the website homepage.
Click on the navigation item labeled "My Schedule," "Planner," or "Scheduling Tools" to open the scheduling section.
Click the button labeled "Create New Weekly Plan" or similar to start a new schedule.

Click the "Add Session" button, choose "Speech Therapy" from the service dropdown, type "Alex" in the child name field, select "Tuesday" in the day-of-week selector, choose "4:00 PM" as the start time, select "60 minutes" as the duration, and then click "Save Session."

Click "Add Session" again, select "Occupational Therapy" as the service, keep the child name as "Alex," select "Thursday" as the day, choose "5:30 PM" as the start time, set the duration to "60 minutes," and click "Save Session."

Click "Add Session" a third time, select "Counseling" from the service dropdown, use child name "Alex," select "Saturday" as the day, choose "11:00 AM" as the start time, set the duration to "45 minutes," and click "Save Session."
Click the "Week View" or "View Weekly Summary" tab or button to open the weekly overview of the created plan.
Click the "Save Plan" button, type "Alex Spring Schedule" into the plan name field in the dialog that appears, and click the "Save" or "Done" button to store the plan.

tasks.name: Submit a one-time $75 pledge to the Therapy Scholarship Fund in honor of a family
tasks.description: Open the donation page, set up a one-time pledge of $75 allocated specifically to the Therapy Scholarship Fund, add an "in honor of" dedication with a specified name, and submit the pledge form with contact details.
steps: Navigate to the website homepage.
Click on the navigation item labeled "Support Us" or "Donate" to open the donation page.
On the donation page, select the option for "One-Time Gift" rather than recurring giving.
In the donation amount field, type "75" to indicate a $75 pledge.
In the "Allocate My Gift" or "Fund" dropdown, select "Therapy Scholarship Fund."
In the dedication section, select the radio button or checkbox labeled "In honor of" and type "The Garcia Family" into the honoree name field.
In the contact information section, enter a full name such as "Morgan Lee," a phone number like "555-987-6543," and an email address such as "morgan@example.com" into the appropriate fields.
In the preferred contact method dropdown, select "Email," then click the "Submit Pledge" or "Send Donation Pledge" button to complete the pledge form.

entities.name: Service
entities.description: Core therapy services offered by the organization (e.g., Speech Therapy, Occupational Therapy, Counseling). Used across booking, groups, planner, and pricing.

fields.name: age_groups
fields.description: Array of age group enums such as ages_3_5, ages_8_10

entities.name: Therapist
entities.description: Individual therapists listed in the directory and used for appointments and favorites.

fields.name: specialties
fields.description: Array of specialty names such as 'Autism', 'Feeding', etc.

fields.name: primary_service_codes
fields.description: Array of Service.code enums for services this therapist provides

fields.name: age_groups_served
fields.description: Array of age group enums such as teens_13_17

fields.name: primary_location_id
fields.description: ClinicLocation.id for the therapist’s main clinic

fields.name: languages
fields.description: Array of language names such as 'English', 'Spanish'

entities.name: ClinicLocation
entities.description: Physical clinic locations used for appointments, groups, and location search.

fields.name: amenities
fields.description: Array of amenity enums such as free_parking, wheelchair_accessible

entities.name: AppointmentSlot
entities.description: Bookable appointment slots for evaluations or therapy sessions, tied to a service and location (and optionally a therapist).

entities.name: AppointmentRequest
entities.description: Requests for individual appointments or evaluations, including slot-based bookings and therapist-preference requests.

fields.name: appointment_slot_id
fields.description: Link to AppointmentSlot when a specific slot is chosen

entities.name: FavoriteTherapist
entities.description: Therapists saved to the single user’s favorites list for quick access and booking.

entities.name: ResourceItem
entities.description: Parent resources (articles, videos, etc.) shown in the resources library and reading list.

fields.name: topic_tags
fields.description: Array of topic tags such as 'sensory processing'

fields.name: age_groups
fields.description: Array of age group enums such as ages_3_5, ages_8_10

entities.name: SavedResource
entities.description: Entries in the single user’s saved reading list (bookmarked resources).

entities.name: GroupProgram
entities.description: Group programs and classes (e.g., social skills groups) listed in the Groups & Classes section.

fields.name: age_groups
fields.description: Array of age group enums such as ages_8_10

entities.name: GroupSessionOption
entities.description: Specific recurring session options for a group program (e.g., 'Tuesdays 4:00 PM').

fields.name: start_time
fields.description: Time of day, e.g., '16:00' for 4:00 PM

entities.name: GroupRegistration
entities.description: Registrations for group programs, including child and parent information.

entities.name: InsurancePlan
entities.description: Insurance plans accepted by the organization and selectable in the cost estimator.

entities.name: ServicePricing
entities.description: Base pricing configuration for each service, used by the cost estimator.

fields.name: in_network_rate_modifier
fields.description: Multiplier to apply for in-network plans, e.g., 0.8

fields.name: out_of_network_rate_modifier
fields.description: Multiplier to apply for out-of-network plans, e.g., 1.0

entities.name: CostEstimate
entities.description: Calculated cost estimates for a given mix of services and an insurance plan.

entities.name: CostEstimateServiceItem
entities.description: Line items within a cost estimate, one per service (e.g., 4 speech sessions, 2 OT sessions).

entities.name: CostEstimateContactRequest
entities.description: Contact requests sent from the cost estimator asking for a detailed monthly quote.

entities.name: CallbackRequest
entities.description: Requests for a clinic to call the parent back from a specific location detail page.

entities.name: WeeklyPlan
entities.description: Named weekly therapy plans for a child, used in the My Planner tool.

entities.name: WeeklyPlanSession
entities.description: Individual therapy sessions within a weekly plan (e.g., Speech on Tuesday at 4:00 PM for 60 minutes).

fields.name: start_time
fields.description: Time of day, e.g., '16:00' for 4:00 PM

entities.name: DonationFund
entities.description: Donation funds (e.g., Therapy Scholarship Fund) available to allocate pledges to.

entities.name: DonationPledge
entities.description: Donation or pledge submissions, including fund allocation and dedication information.
